title: BDD Tier 1: executable CLI scenarios for skill-pouring
description: Real TDD. Scenarios: bootstrap pours skills (tree+byte-equality); update re-pours (idempotent byte+mtime, removes managed-but-removed, never touches shop-owned); BC shop receives the BC skill set.
close_reason: CLI iter_skill_files + bootstrap pour + update mirror (idempotent/prune), bc-only; 6 new scenarios green, no regressions vs base

title: CLI: pour templates/skills into BC on bootstrap + update
description: Add a templates/skills/** -> <target>/.claude/skills/ pour to bootstrap and update. Managed/reconciled like agent files: re-pour on update, leave byte+mtime unchanged when identical, remove managed-but-removed skills, never touch shop-owned files. Add a pure importlib.resources accessor (no working-dir path). TDD: failing executable scenarios first. Currently NO mechanism pours skills (the TDD bake is inert).
close_reason: CLI iter_skill_files + bootstrap pour + update mirror (idempotent/prune), bc-only; 6 new scenarios green, no regressions vs base

title: Author/adapt the BC skill set under templates/skills/
description: Vendor & adapt skills: bc-router (new), bc-sufficiency-check (new, from role prose), writing-plans (beads-backed, no doc, BDD-aware), test-driven-development (make mandatory), subagent-driven-development (adapted), using-git-worktrees (adapted), integrating-to-main (from finishing-a-development-branch), bc-review (adversarial), work-done-gate (3 pre-emit checks + emit). Many upstream SKILL.md files need modification.

title: Bake adapted test-driven-development skill into bc-implementer role template (lead-bgdc)
description: Ship test-driven-development/SKILL.md + testing-anti-patterns.md as package data co-located so @-reference resolves; add additive skill reference to bc-implementer template body. Constraints: scenarios 02 (byte-for-byte show), 04 (required sections), 48 (Your-job phrasing), and list (four names) must still pass.
close_reason: Shipped test-driven-development skill (SKILL.md + testing-anti-patterns.md) as package data under templates/skills/; added additive 'Skill — Test-Driven Development' section to bc-implementer template; pyproject package-data glob updated. Scenarios 02/04/48/list all pass; baseline 4 unrelated failures unchanged.

title: BDD Tier 2: content-invariant scenarios for new skill structure
description: Guardrails, semantic (not literal-header). Pin: router classifies all 3 message types to correct gate path; TDD mandatory (no self-granted exception); work-done-gate carries the 3 pre-emit checks; writing-plans beads-backed+BDD-aware; implementer never emits scenario work_done / reviewer sole emitter.

title: Rewrite bc-primer / CLAUDE wording to router-first operating model
description: Update .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md (and the shipped claude/bc.md body if needed) so the BC operating model is router-skill-first: main agent loads bc-router, which classifies and dispatches to role subagents.

title: Rewrite bc-implementer.md / bc-reviewer.md as thin bias-shims
description: Shrink both role templates to bias-carrying shims that compose the shared skills. implementer bias=make-it-real-via-TDD, never emits scenario work_done; reviewer bias=adversarial gate, sole emitter. Add model: frontmatter so each can target a different model.

title: Refactor BC templates to superpowers-style skill architecture
description: Replace the flat-prose BC role prompts (bc-implementer ~475 lines, bc-reviewer) with composable superpowers-style skills driven by a top-level bc-router skill and executed via subagent-driven development with mandatory TDD. Preserve the lead<->BC wire contract (accept messages -> work to done -> emit work_done). BC scope only; lead templates out of scope. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-bc-superpowers-skills-design.md. All work on experimental/superpowers-bc-skills.
notes: Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-bc-superpowers-skills.md. Phases A(CLI pour, TDD)->B(skills)->C(shims)->D(primer)->E(Tier2 guardrails)->F(transition). Tracker of record = beads.

title:
EXPERIMENTAL — Slice 1 of the TDD-skill integration initiative. Integrate faithfully; flag via `clarify` anything about the template mechanism that does not fit. We expect to learn and refine.

## Goa
Release workflow emits repository_dispatch to bc-launcher on version-tag release (lead-jx4u / scenario 26ca8a14e01db50c)
description: On a version-tag release of shopsystem-templates, its release workflow must perform a repository_dispatch API call targeting the shopsystem-bc-launcher repository, carrying a credential authorized to dispatch. Pin via Gherkin scenario 26ca8a14e01db50c; add feature file, conftest step defs, and the .github/workflows release workflow.
notes: Implementer turn complete (lead-jx4u). Wrote features/release_workflow_repository_dispatch_to_bc_launcher.feature (hash 26ca8a14e01db50c), added conftest step defs, implemented .github/workflows/release.yml (version-tag trigger -> repository_dispatch to shopsystem-bc-launcher via secrets.BC_LAUNCHER_DISPATCH_TOKEN). New scenario passes. 4 pre-existing unrelated CLAUDE.md/messaging-encapsulation failures confirmed present on clean tree (out of scope). Handing off to bc-reviewer; not closing.
close_reason: work_done complete signed off for lead-jx4u; scenario 26ca8a14e01db50c passing on origin/main @67f3066

title: Transition handling for old structure-pinning scenarios
description: Keep both during transition: old prose-structure scenarios (bc_implementer_sections, bc_reviewer_sections, *_cli_naming, prose pre_emit_*) remain (failing/xfail) alongside new scenarios until the design settles, then clean up. Preserves a visible diff.
